question: Bills passed by the legislature usually requires the approval of the following except_______
solution:
A bill is usually considered and voted on by members of the House of Representatives, but it does not require their approval again after being passed. The bill must go through a formal process that involves both chambers of the legislature and then requires the executive's approval.

In a federal system like that of the United States or Nigeria, a bill passed by the national legislature (e.g., Congress or the National Assembly) requires the president's assent to become law. Also, in many countries, especially in federal systems like Nigeria, a bill passed by the state legislature requires the governor's assent to become law. In some countries with a constitutional monarchy, such as the United Kingdom, bills require royal assent from the monarch.

solution: The independence of the judiciary means that the courts must be free from interference by the executive and legislative arms of government. This ensures that judges make decisions based on law and justice, not political pressure or external influence.

question: The last measure used by the pressure groups issuing a condition to be satisfied within a stipulated time is called_______
solution: Pressure groups commonly issued deadlines(ultimatum) for the government to fulfill their requests and queries before embarking on strike action.

question: The non-stratified society in the pre-colonial era was that of the ........ where decisions were made________
solution: The Igbo precolonial was seen as ''headless'' and decisions were made based on popular consultations and acceptability.

question: The Brussel conference in 1889/90 was based on______

option.a: putting an end to slave trade
option.c: furthering the decision on colonialism as previously discussed in the Berlin conference
option.d: deciding on how to penetrate Africa for economic adventurism

solution: The Brussel Conference unlike the Berlin Conference was targeted at the reconsideration of the unjust virtues and vices occasioned by colonialism, thereby specifically seeking to eradicate slave trade and related activities.

question: In the all-Nigerian constitutional conference of 1950_______

option.a: all official members of the legislative council participated
option.b: it was resolved that the Nigerians federation be organized on ethnic basis
option.c: it was concluded that a quasi-federal structure should be developed for colonial Nigeria
option.d: all unofficial members of the legislative council participated

solution: Recognizing and acknowledging the relative importance of direct participation and contribution by people in their own affairs, the All-Nigerian Constitutional Conference marked an unprecendented attendance of all previously relegated unofficial members of the local legislature.

question: Implementations of the target programmes of the government is the primary duty of the _____ a part of the _____
solution: The Civil Service is an integral branch of the public service which primarily functions to implement the policies of the government.

question: The federal judge under the 1963 constitution could be removed by the______
solution: 2/3 majority support or vote of the National Assembly members was enough under the 1963 Constitution to remove a federal judge.

question: Which of the following was not responsible for the coalition between A. G and N. C. N. C.?

option.b: Awolowo's faction seeking to get N. P. C. out of power as opposed to Akintola's option
option.c: contest for the federal election of 1964
option.d: Akintola's faction which broke out of A. G. to form UPP

solution: All the following options facilitated the coalition of the action Group and the National Council of the Nigerian Citizens except the The Kano Riot of 1953

question: The most representative organ of the united nations is the_______
solution: General Assembly. The UN General Assembly comprises the Heads of State and Government of all the State Parties in the UN body.

question: Executive Council of the African Union makes decision by_______

option.d: the veto of the General Secretariat

solution: The Council of Ministers of the State Parties of the AU, the Executive Council makes decision primarily by consensus or by 2/3 majority support.

question: Amendment of the U. N. O. charter is the duty of the ........
solution: The UN General Assembly has the duty of amending relevant provisions of the UN Charter.

question: The delineation of electoral constituencies for reasons of political advantage is termed___________
solution: Gerrymandering is the process of creating political constituencies in order to favour the electoral scores of a political party.

question: In most recent times, a seemingly workable solution to the Nigerian federal question is that of_______
solution: Although Options A, B and C are recurring options for the Nigerian federalism, the clarion call for restructuring is louder than ever before.

question: The concept of delegated legislation is often criticised for all but one of the following reasons?

option.a: it may lead to abuse of the powers
option.b: for reasons of sheer ignorance on legislative substance and procedure
option.c: for reasons of expertise rule making on specific subject matters
option.d: it is a clear violation of the separation of powers concept

solution: Delegated Legislation is supported largely because certain specialist idea is needed for the purpose of rule making on areas upon which the legislature may not have sufficient knowledge and skill in. Example, the INEC is empowered to make election guidelines and regulations aside the Electoral Act and in furtherance of its duties.

question: ____ is noted for having said "man by nature is a political animal"
solution: According to Aristotle, every human interaction intersects with political determinations and interests.

question: Class stratification is a peculiar feature of___________
solution: In a capitalist system, there is a clear differentiation gap between the ruling class and the ruled or the proletarates.

question: A constitution is a legal document _________

option.c: forming the basis upon which a government rules the country
option.d: only likely to succeed in countries where there is union government

solution: A constitution is defined as a body of agreed rules and principles stating how the powers of governing a country are given and how these powers are to be exercised.

question: A government in which control of ultimate power is by a few who rule in their selfish interest is classified as _________
solution: An oligarchy is a form of government that places power in the hands of a few people.s

question: A system of government in which power derives from total control of the instruments of force is called ___________
solution: Facism is a form of government headed by a dictator, in which government has a total control over all activities in the state.

question: The British Government revoked the charter of the Royal Niger Company and took over the direct administration of Nigeria in _________
solution: On 1 January 1900, the Royal Niger Company transferred its territories to the British Government. This change marked the beginning of direct British colonial rule over Nigeria, transitioning from the earlier system of indirect control through the Royal Niger Company.

question: Warrant Chiefs were appointed to __________
solution: Colonial authorities selected some people whom they made chiefs and were given certificate of recognition. They were referred to as warrant chief who took charge of  local administration

question: The application of the rule of law may be constrained by ________

option.a: securing the tenure of office of judges
option.c: employing men of proven integrity as judges

solution: The rule of law can be constrained by invoking emergency powers. For instance, if there is a war, a state of emergency could be declared. Bad leaders may be enthroned and citizens may be denied of some of their rights.

question: The Gulf War of 1990 is an indication that the U. N. O. is unable to _______
solution: The Gulf War of 1990 (also known as the First Gulf War) was a conflict where a U.S.-led coalition, backed by the United Nations (U.N.), fought against Iraq after it invaded Kuwait. Despite the U.N.'s efforts to maintain peace, war still broke out, proving that the U.N. cannot ensure permanent world peace.

question: The economic basis of feudalism is __________
solution: Feudalism was a system that primarily relied on agriculture as its economic foundation, with the majority of the population engaged in farming to support their livelihoods and those of their lords.

question: The Premier of Western Region immediately after independence was _________
solution: The Premier of Western Region immediately after independence was S.L. Akintola.  He became the Premier 1960, however, there was a crisis between Akinotla and Awolowo , Moses Majekodunmi was then appointed as a Sole administrator until 1963 Akintola resumed as the Premier of Western Region

question: The lbadan Conference of 1950 was convened to discuss the provisions of the ....
solution: The Ibadan Conference of 1950 was convened to discuss the provisions of the Macpherson Constitution. Sir John Macphereson became Government of Nigeria in 1948. He then took immediate steps to review Sir Arthur Richard's Constitution of 1946 and fashioned a new constitution which came into being in 1951.

question: The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about …
solution: The absence of the rule of law in government will bring about human rights abuse. This is so as the rule of law refers to the constitutional provision which emphasizes the supremacy of the law, the equality before the law and existence of fundamental human rights.

question: During the Civil War, the major power that expressed moral support for Biafra's self- determination was ......
solution:
During the Civil War, the major power that expressed moral support for Biafra's self-determination was France.

France gave both financial and material assistance to Biafra.

question: After 1945, the demand of African nationalists changed from reform to independence because .....

option.a: The Second World War enhanced colonial rule
option.d: The Second World War boosted their morale

solution: After 1945, the demand of African nationalists changed from reform to independence because the second world war boosted their morale. The war exposed the myth surrounding white supremacy.

question: ____________was the method used by the British to facilitate the administration of Southern Nigeria.
solution: Divide and rule policy, or divide and conquer, is gaining and maintaining power by breaking up larger concentrations of power into pieces that individually have less power than the one implementing the strategy.

question: ________refers to the manipulation of boundaries of constituencies in order to win more seats,
solution: In representative democracies, Gerrymandering refers to political manipulation of electoral district boundaries with the intent of creating undue advantage for a party, group, or socio-economic class within the constituency

question: Financial allocation to local governments by the federal or the state government to supplement the cost of a project is called_____
solution:
A matching grant is a financial contribution provided by a higher level of government (federal or state) to local governments to help cover the costs of specific projects. The key aspect of a matching grant is that it requires the local government to contribute a certain amount of their own funds to receive the grant. This is done to ensure that the local government is also invested in the project and to encourage efficient use of resources.

- Revenue allocation refers to the distribution of revenue (usually taxes) collected by the government among various levels of government or different sectors.

- Reimbursement is the repayment of funds spent by an individual or organisation, typically for expenses already incurred.

- Statutory Allocation involves the mandatory distribution of funds based on laws or regulations, often involving pre-determined formulas or criteria.

question: The political rights of a citızen include the
solution: The right to vote is one of the political rights of a citizen. A citizen who is of legal age must be allowed to vote in any election.

question: An electoral system in which the winner must secure more than 50% of the vote is described as a/an
solution: The electoral system in which the winner must secure more than 50% of the vote is described as an "absolute majority," This means the candidate or option needs to obtain more than half of the total votes cast to win.

question: A one-party system helps to prevent
solution: One of the merits of a one-party system is that it helps to prevent economic waste. With only one party in a state, resources do not have to be diverted to conducting elaborate elections in the selection of leaders.

question: The highest body which performed legislative functions under Babangida Regime was
solution:
The Supreme Military Council, SMC was the top highest organ of the Federal Government of Nigeria in the military regimes of Johnson Aguiyi-Ironsi, General Yakubu Gowon, Murtala Mohammed, General Obasanjo and Major General Muhammadu Buhari.
The body was instituted in 1966 by the administration of Aguiyi Ironsi deriving power from statute 8 of decree 1, it was later replaced as the highest body in a military regime by President Ibrahim Babangida in 1985 who created the Armed Forces Ruling Council.

question: One of these is a major problem of the Nigerian civil service
solution: Corruption is widely recognized as one of the major problems of the Nigerian civil service. It manifests in various forms such as embezzlement, bribery, nepotism, and mismanagement of public funds, which significantly hinder efficiency and service delivery.

question: The defence pact between Nigeria and Britain was cancelled in the year
solution: The defence pact between Nigeria and Britain, known as the Anglo-Nigerian Defense Pact, was indeed cancelled in 1962. This agreement, signed in 1960 shortly after Nigeria gained independence from Britain, allowed British military presence and assistance in Nigeria. However, Nigeria sought to assert its sovereignty more fully, leading to negotiations in 1962 that resulted in the cancellation of the pact. This move was part of Nigeria's broader efforts to establish its independence and autonomy in foreign and defence policies following decolonization.

question: A recurring principal objective of Nigerian foreign policy is
solution:
A recurring principal objective of Nigeria's foreign policy has been the promotion of world peace with justice, especially through African unity, regional stability, and global cooperation. Nigeria's foreign policy is centered on:

Respect for international law and justice
Commitment to global peace and security
Promotion of African unity and economic development
Non-alignment and peaceful resolution of conflicts

question: The operations of a pressure group can be hindered by
solution: Poor leadership can lead to ineffective decision-making, lack of clear direction, and internal conflicts, all of which can hinder the operations of a pressure group.

question: The 1976 Local Government Reforms made the Nigerian local government become
solution: The 1976 Local Government Reforms in Nigeria made significant changes to the local government system. The reforms aimed to make local governments more autonomous by granting them more powers and responsibilities.

question: Unitary system of government is best applicable to a/an
solution: An homogeneous state has a single language, belief, culture and ethnicity among its people.

question: The ________ as a new policy direction introduced by the French government that was aimed at granting more autonomy to its colonies under pressure from independence movements.
solution: The loi-cadre ( Reform Act) was a French legal reform passed by the French National Assembly on 23 June 1956. It marked a turning point in relations between France and its overseas empire. Under pressure from independence movements in the colonies, the government transferred a number of powers from Paris to elected territorial governments in French African colonies and also removed remaining voting inequalities by implementing universal suffrage and abolishing the multiple electoral college system.

question: On 1 October 1995, Abacha announced a three-year program for return to civilian rule. Parties were allowed to form in July 1998. Which of the following parties was not registered by the Provisional Ruling Council?
solution: Three parties were registered by the Provisional Ruling Council for participation in local, state and national elections: the All People's Party or APP led by Mahmud Waziri; the People's Democratic Party or PDP led by Soloman Lar; and the Alliance for Democracy or AD, led by Ayo Adebanjo.

question: The process of pressure groups trying to influence legislation is known as
solution: Pressure groups are organisations which seek to influence the policies of the government and this process is known as lobbying.

question: Which one of these served as secretary general of the League of nations?
solution: Joseph Avenol (1879--1952) was a French diplomat who served as the secretary-general of the League of Nations in the years leading up to World War II. He succeeded British diplomat, Eric Drummond.

question: Which of the following is a fundamental principle of democratic governance?
solution: Citizen Participation is a fundamental principle of democratic governance. In a democracy, citizens have the right to participate in decision-making processes that affect their lives and their community. This participation can take various forms, including voting in elections, running for office, participating in civic groups, and voicing their opinions through protest and dialogue.

question: Which of the following is not a source of public opinion?
solution: Academic Journals are not a source of public opinion. They are scholarly publications that focus on academic or scientific research, and while they may contain articles that analyze public opinion, they are not themselves a source of public opinion. Public opinion is typically gauged through methods like opinion polls, social media analysis, and the platforms of political parties.

question: In a parliamentary system of government, the head of government is typically ___________.

option.b: the leader of the majority party in the legislature
option.d: chosen by the head of state

solution: In a parliamentary system of government, the head of government is typically the leader of the majority party in the legislature. This is because the head of government, often called the prime minister, is usually the leader of the political party that has the most seats in the legislature.

question: The constitutional amendment process in Nigeria requires the approval of
solution: The constitutional amendment process in Nigeria requires the approval of the National Assembly. The National Assembly, which consists of the Senate and the House of Representatives, has the power to alter the provisions of the constitution. The President, the Supreme Court, and the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) do not have the power to approve constitutional amendments.

question: During the cold war, Nigeria's foreign policy was characterized by
solution: During the cold war, Nigeria's foreign policy was characterized by Neutrality and non-alignment. This is because Nigeria did not want to be seen as taking sides in the ideological war between the United States and the Soviet Union. Instead, Nigeria pursued a policy of neutrality and non-alignment, focusing on its own national interests and the promotion of peace and stability in Africa.

question: Which of the following is a right associated with citizenship?
solution: The right to vote is a right associated with citizenship. This is because voting is a fundamental way in which citizens participate in the democratic process. Other rights associated with citizenship include the right to run for public office, the right to work, and the right to receive government services.

question: Which African country successfully resisted European imperialism and maintained its independence?
solution: Ethiopia is the African country that successfully resisted European imperialism and maintained its independence. Ethiopia was able to maintain its independence after defeating Italy at the Battle of Adwa in 1896.

question: Pressure groups primarily aim to

option.c: influence public opinion through media campaigns
option.d: disrupt the functioning of the government

solution: Pressure groups primarily aim to influence public opinion through media campaigns. They use various forms of advocacy to shape the perceptions and attitudes of the public towards certain issues, with the goal of influencing policy and decision-making in their favor.

question: A key principle of the socialist system of government is the ___________.

option.a: minimal government intervention in the economy
option.b: private ownership of the means of production
option.c: power concentrated in the hands of a few elite individuals

solution: A key principle of the socialist system of government is the equality of opportunity and outcome. This means that in a socialist system, the government controls the means of production and wealth is distributed equally among the population to ensure everyone has the same opportunities and outcomes.

question: In a communist system, who owns and controls the means of production?
solution: In a communist system, the means of production are owned and controlled by the government. This is because communism advocates for a classless society where wealth and power are distributed equally among the citizens. Therefore, the government controls the means of production to ensure this equal distribution.

question: Which of the following was a significant trade commodity in the pre-colonial Kingdom of Benin?
solution: Ivory was a significant trade commodity in the pre-colonial Kingdom of Benin. The Kingdom of Benin, located in modern-day Nigeria, was known for its ivory carvings, which were traded with European merchants.

question: In a parliamentary system, the head of government is usually the
solution: In a parliamentary system, the head of government is usually the Prime Minister. The Prime Minister is the leader of the political party that has the most seats in the parliament. The Prime Minister is responsible for running the government and implementing its policies. The President or the Monarch, if there is one, is usually the head of state and performs ceremonial duties.

question: A major event in Nigerian history that led to the suspension of Nigeria's constitution and the declaration of a state of emergency by the colonial authorities in 1953 was the ________.
solution: The Kano riots were a series of violent clashes in Northern Nigeria in 1953. The riots were sparked by political and ethnic tensions over proposed reforms in the Northern House of Assembly. The British colonial authorities suspended Nigeria's constitution and declared a state of emergency to restore order, leading to the dissolution of democratic regional governments. The Kano riots highlighted the challenges Nigeria faced during the transition from colonial rule to self-governance.

question: Which of the following countries operate a direct democracy?
solution: Switzerland operates a direct democracy. In a direct democracy, citizens have the right to vote on every issue that affects them, rather than their elected representatives making those decisions. This is different from a representative democracy, where citizens elect representatives who make decisions on their behalf, which is the system used in the United Kingdom, United States, and Russia.

question: Nigeria shares its longest border with which West African country?
solution: Nigeria shares its longest border with Niger. The border between Nigeria and Niger is approximately 1,497 kilometers long, making it Nigeria's longest international border.

question: The Ijaw people of pre-colonial Nigeria were organized into?
solution: The Ijaw people of pre-colonial Nigeria were organized into clans. A clan is a group of people united by actual or perceived kinship and descent. In the case of the Ijaw people, these clans were often made up of several villages and communities that shared common ancestry.

question: Nigeria's foreign policy is guided by the principle of
solution: Nigeria's foreign policy is guided by the principle of national interest. This means that the country's foreign relations and actions are primarily based on what is beneficial or of interest to the nation. This could be in terms of economic, political, security, or other national considerations.

question: The Nigerian constitution guarantees freedom of speech under which fundamental right?
solution: The Nigerian constitution guarantees freedom of speech under the Right to freedom of expression. This right allows individuals to express their thoughts, ideas, and opinions without fear of government restraint.

question: The productivity of public corporations is believed to be enhanced when there is
solution: Public Corporation operates better when there is little to no political interference. This is because it allows the corporation to function like a business rather than a government body, reduce corruption and improve accountability. Measures such as privatisation, commercialisation and deregulation can be taken to reduce political interference

question: NEPAD stands for

option.a: New Economic Programmes for African Development

solution:
NEPAD stands for New Partnerships for Africa's Development. It is a home-grown African initiative to overcome the age-long obstacles to Africa's development. The body partners with a group of 8 industrialised countries:

1. The Russian Federation

2. Germany

3. France

4 Italy

5. Japan

6. United Kingdom

7. Northern Ireland

8. United States of America

question: Nigerian Ports Authority is an example of
solution: The Nigerian Port Authority is an example of a public corporation. A public corporation is a government organisation that provides essential services to the public. The Nigerian Ports Authority is responsible for providing smooth unloading and offloading of exported and imported cargo.

question: An agent in which the state undertakes direct political socialisation is the
solution: The state undertakes direct political socialisation through the schools. This is done through the Ministry of Education that provides curriculums that contain subjects such as Civic Education, Government, Security Education, etc to ensure that pupils/ students become aware of the political situation and understand the values, beliefs and attitudes shared by members of the society.

question: The Nigerian Youth Movement pursued its objectives through the use of
solution: The Nigerian Youth Movement, like most early political parties, pursued its objectives through the use of newspapers. They established their ideologies and aired their opinions concerning political issues through newspapers.

question: The commander of the army under the Hausa/ Fulani pre-colonial administration was
solution: The madawaki was the commander of the army in Pre-colonial Hausa/ Fulani Empire.

question: A naturalised citizen can lose his citizenship if found guilty of
solution: Treason is the crime of betraying one's own country. A naturalised citizen found guilty of treason will lose his/ her citizenship.

question: Shehu Shagari won the 1979 Presidential Election under
solution: Shehu Shagari won the 1979 Presidential Election under the umbrella of the National Party of Nigeria (NPN). It also who the 1983 Presidential Election which did not take effect because of the transition to military rule.

question: Absence of a Red-tape is a merit of
solution: Red-tape refers to time-consuming regulations or bureaucratic procedures. The absence of red-tape is one of the merits of a unitary structure. WIthout the several levels that accompanies a federal structure (central, state and local) or a confederal state (central and autonomous unit), decisions are made faster without the need for processing and approval from a higher level.

question: The Nigerian Civil War was fought during the military regime of
solution: The Nigerian Civil War took place between 1967-70 under the regime of General Yakubu Gowon (1966-75). It was a war between Eastern Nigeria (Biafra) and Nigeria.

question: Which local government reform in Nigeria prevented traditional rulers from engaging in politics?
solution: The 1976 Reform of the Local Government in Nigeria changed the multiple system of local government councils to a single-tier all-purpose local government. It also led to the exclusion of traditional rulers from local government councils.

question: A union of states with a common recognised authority is
solution: A federal system is a system whereby power is shared among the federal level and the component units. The component units (the states) report to a common authority at the centre (the federal level).

question: Aggregate view of individuals in a society is referred to as
solution: Public opinion is the beliefs, values and attitudes, which are commonly held and expressed by the majority of the people on a given public issue.

question: Which of these colonial constitutions in Nigeria endured for the longest period?
solution: The Clifford Constitution is the colonial constitution that lasted for the longest period (1922-46). The Richards Constitution was adopted in 1946. The Macpherson Constitution was adopted in 1951 while the Lyttleton Constitution was adopted in 1954.

question: One of these is not an organ of the Commonwealth of Nations

option.a: Assembly of Heads of State and Government

solution: The Assembly of Heads of State and Government is not an organ of the Commonwealth but rather an organ of the African Union.

question: Price stability in the international oil market is the major objective of the
solution: The major objective of the Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is to ensure price stability in the international oil market. This is to ensure that fluctuation is eliminated and that every member-state can benefit from oil sales.

question: The June 12 election in Nigeria was annulled by the regime of
solution: The Presidential Election of 1993 which was supposedly meant to transfer power back to civilian rule from the hands of Ibrahim Babangida was contested by Alhaji Tofa of the NRC and Moshood K.O. Abiola of the SDP. The Armed Forces Ruling Council (AFRC) then annulled the entire exercise.

question: The manipulation of boundaries of constituencies in order to win more seats is called
solution:
The manipulation of boundaries of constituencies in order to win more seats is called gerrymandering.

According to Ofoegbu (1999) Gerry-mandering constituencies is the process of deliminating constituencies to favour a particular political party or candidate in the election.

question: One argument against a multi-party system is thes
solution:
One argument against a mult-party system is the high cost of conducting election. It is very expensive in both men and materials.

It should be noted that encouragement of opposition and instability is also correct. The unhealthy rivalry, frequent political re-alignment and coalition make government of a country to be weak and unstable.

question: The method used by the British to facilitate the administration of Southern Nigeria was
solution:
The method used by the British to facilitate the administration of Southern Nigeria was divide and rule.
The system brought about division between the traditional rulers who were looked upon a British puppets and the alienated educated elites thereby, strengthening the policy divide and rule.

question: After 1945, the demand of African nationalists changed from reform to independence because

option.c: the second world war boosted their morale
option.d: the second world war enhanced colonial rule.

solution:
After 1945, the demand of African nationalists changed from reform to independence because the second World War boosted their morale.

The war exposed the myth surrounding white supremacy. The ex-soldiers from West African saw that the whites were not as strong as they portrayed themselves to Nigerians and in fact that coming back from the war they assorted their rights and joined the nationalists in the struggle for independence.

question: When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the head of state was the
solution:
When Nigeria achieved independence in 1960, the Head of state was the Governor-General, representing the queen of England.
One of the main provisions of the constitution of 1960 was that the governor general was made the constitutional ceremonial Head of state and a representative of the Queen of England.

This means the queen was the head of state but appointed a representative in the person of the governor-general to stand in for her, in her absence.

question: Under the 1963 constitution, items not listed in the exclusive and concurrent list were within the exclusive competence of the
solution:
Under the 1963 constitution, items not listed in the exclusive and concurrent list were within the exclusive competence of the regional legislatures.Sir Oliver Lyttleton constitution of 1954 classified government powers into three:
1. Exclusive powers - matters where only the federal or central government can legislate only.
2. Concurrent powers - matters where both the federal government and regional (state) can legislate and
Residual 0powers reserved for the regional legislature only.

question: Before Nigeria became a republic, the highest body charged with the administrator of justice was the
solution: Before Nigeria became a republic, the highest body charged with the administration of justice was the Privy council. one of the features of the Republican Constitution of 1963 is that the supreme court of Nigeria became the highest court in Nigeria. Appeal cases stopped going to the privy council in London

question: The main source of financing local government in Nigeria is
solution: In Nigeria, the main source of financing for local governments is statutory revenue allocation. This refers to the funds allocated to local governments by the federal government, which come from a share of the country's national revenue. These allocations are distributed based on the principles set out in the Nigerian Constitution and the revenue-sharing formula.

question: The most remarkable legacy of the 1976 Local Government Reform in Nigeria was the introduction of
solution:
The most remarkable legacy of the 1976 Local Government Reform in Nigeria was the introduction of uniformity in structure and functions.

In February 1976 Federal Military Government issued a degree establishing a uniform Local Government Structure throughout the country. The measure is significant in a away in the sense that the first time in the history of Nigeria a national rather than Region/state approach was adopted toward Local Government.

question: The major factor that influenced the formulation of Nigerian foreign policy in the First Republic was
solution:
The major factor that influenced the formulation of Nigerian foreign policy in the First Republic was the colonial legacy.

According to J.U. Anyaele (1994), we can rightly agree that the colonial legacy fore-ordained our foreign policy initially. This is one of the major reasons why the Balewa Administration maintains close relations mainly with Britain and other commonwealth countries.

question: South-South cooperation is a major policy plan on which Nigeria bases her relations with
solution: South-South cooperation refers to the collaboration between countries in the Global South, primarily focused on mutual development, technical exchange and sharing of resources and knowledge to address common challenges. Nigeria, as a developing country, engages in South-South cooperation with other developing nations, rather than with developed countries.

question: Nigeria's recognition of the Popular Movement for the Liberation of Angola to lead that country was made under
solution: Under General Murtala Mohammed's regime, Nigeria adopted an assertive foreign policy supporting liberation movements. During his short tenure in 1975–1976, Nigeria recognized the Popular Movement for the Liberation of Angola (MPLA) as the legitimate leadership of Angola when it gained independence.

question: Nigeria's withdrawal from the Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in July 1986 was in protest against British

option.a: support for UNITA rebels in Angola
option.c: failure to impose sanctions on South Africa

solution:
Nigeria's withdrawal from the Edinburgh Commonwealth Games in July 1986 was in protest against British failure to impose sanctions on South Africa.


She, Britain did not support but in Apartheid regime in South Africa.

question: Which of the following is the function of the Council of the Ministers of the O.A.U

option.a: Cordinating of the general policy of the organization
option.b: Directing the finances of the organization
option.c: Preparing the agenda of the organization's meetings
option.d: Reviewing the functions and activities of other organs of the organizations

solution:
Preparing the agenda of the organisation's meeting is the function of the council of Ministers of the O.A.U.

The Council of Ministers is the body which is composed of the foreign ministers of member countries. it meets about twice a year but extra-ordinary meetings may be called if and such a request is approved by two-thirds of the members.

One of the responsibilities is preparing the agenda for the summit i.e the Assembly of Heads of State and Government

question: Citizenship in a modern state expresses the status of a person who possesses
solution:
Citizenship in a modern state expresses the status of a person who possesses full political rights.

This is so as a person who is a legal member of a state is citizen of that state. He must also owe allegiance to that particular state he resides and then enjoys full civil and political rights and will be ready to put his life at stake in defence of that country whenever occasion calls for that

question: Communism is a system which recognizes
solution:
Communism is a system which recognizes the existence of the state.

This is so as communism may be defined as an economic system and a system of government where the means of production, distribution and exchange are totally controlled by the state.

Some features of communism are as follows:

1. Total ownership of means of production and distribution, by the state.

2. Absence of classes, ie classes society.

3. Centrally planned economy.

4. Authoritarian and Totalitarian government

question: The delineation of constituencies is a major duty of the
solution: The delineation of constituencies, which means drawing electoral boundaries, is a key function of the Electoral Commission. In Nigeria, the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) is responsible for reviewing and defining electoral constituencies in accordance with constitutional guidelines and demographic changes.

question: A common feature of a multi-party system is that government is formed by

option.d: the party with the highest votes.

solution:
A common feature of a multi-party system is that government is formed by a coalition of political parties.

When there are several political parties in a country, it then become difficult for one party to win a clear majority in parliament. The part that has won the scanty majority will be forced to form a coalition government with another part. e.g. NPN and NNP during the second Republic.

question: The final interpretation pf the provisions of a federal constitution is vested in the
solution:
The final interpretation pf the provisions of a federal constitution is vested in the highest court of the land.

One of the main characteristics of a Federal System of Government is the existence of a court to interpret the constitution and resolve conflicts between the centre and the federating units.

question: A major issue that distinguishes pressure groups from political parties is
solution:
The objective is a major issue that distinguishes pressure groups from political parties.

Political parties aim at contesting and winning elections and controlling government while pressure groups aim at pursing the interest of their members but do not attempt to control government machinery directly by contesting elections.

question: The application of the principle of separation of powers seems impracticable because powers is
solution: Although the principle of separation of powers calls for distinct branches of government, in practice, these powers are fused. For example, in parliamentary systems the executive is drawn from the legislature, which blurs the separation between branches.

question: A law passed by the legislature and assented by the executive is
solution:
A law passed by the legislature and assented by the executive is an act.

Decree is a law made by the Federal Military Government.

State And People by J.O. Murray (1961) defined Act as the name given to a Bill after it has passed through Parliament and become law. For instance Parliament. Act, 1911 (in British Constitution) which restricted the powers of the House of Lords.

question: One of the features of a sovereign state is that it

option.a: has the capacity to defend itself from external aggression
option.b: has a large number of soldiers
option.c: practises the presidential system of government
option.d: is not indebted to other countries

solution:
One of the features of a Sovereign state is that it has the capacity to defend itself from external aggression.

It must have supreme power to enable it make and enforce laws A Sovereign State must be free from any form of external control eg. Political, military, economic and social control.

question: The main duty of the Local Government Service is to

option.a: supervise and manage the affairs of local governments
option.b: create job opportunities at the local level
option.c: handle requests for the creation of more local governments

solution:
The main duty of the local Government Service Commission is to supervise and manage the affairs of local governments.

The Local Government Service Commission was established in each state of the Federation in 1976 to control all services in the local governments e.g recruitment, promotion, discipline etc. of staff of the local governments.

question: In Nigeria, the Council of State was first created by
solution: The Council of State in Nigeria was first created by General Murtala Muhammed. In a broadcast on 30 July 1975—shortly after deposing General Yakubu Gowon—he introduced the idea and structure of the National Council of State. This body was established to advise the executive on various national issues and has evolved over time through constitutional reforms.

question: One major factor that differentiates the presidential from the parliamentary system is
solution:
Separation of powers is one major factor that differentiates the presidential from the parliamentary system.

In a Presidential system, there is strict separation of powers as the President and the ministers are not members of Parliament while on the Parliamentary System, there is much fusion of powers. Here the Prime Minister and the Cabinet members are members of the Parliament.

question: The greatest criticism of the Security Council of the UNO is that it
solution: One of the most significant criticisms of the UN Security Council is that its structure gives the five permanent members (the United States, the United Kingdom, France, Russia, and China) the power to veto any substantive resolution. This "exclusive veto power" can block action even when there is broad international support for a measure, which many argue hampers the Council's ability to respond effectively to international crises and conflicts. Critics contend that this arrangement often leads to inaction and prevents the Security Council from reflecting the current global political landscape.

question: Which of the following is a founding member of OPEC?
solution:
Venezuela is a founding member of OPEC.

Other founding members are Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Saudi Arabia.

The Organization was formed in September 1960.

question: Who among the following served as Secretary General of OPEC?
solution: Rilwanu Lukman, a Nigerian engineer and technocrat, served as the Secretary General of OPEC from 1995 to 2000. During his tenure, he played a crucial role in managing the organization’s policies and navigating the complexities of global oil markets. His leadership is often noted for its contributions to stabilizing oil production levels and addressing the challenges faced by member countries.

question: Which of these international finance agencies is Nigeria a member of
solution:
Nigeria is member of the International Monetary Fund.

IMF is one of Socialized Agencies of U.N.O and Nigeria being automatically a member of IMF. In addition, Nigeria has been able to obtain loans and aid through IMF as a member.

question: Nigeria's membership of the ECOWAS is informed by her desire to

option.a: develop a market in the sub-region

solution:
Nigeria's membership of the ECOWAS is informed by her desire to promote economic integration.

It should be noted that one of the aims and objectives of ECOWAS is the promotion of cooperation and development in all areas of economic activity, agriculture, natural resources, energy, transport commerce, monetary and financial matters, Social and cultural matters.

question: Government by the wealthy is known as
solution: Government by the wealthy is known as plutocracy it is one of the forms of oligarchy which is defined as government ruled by few privileged and powerful people.

question: The best form of government for a heterogeneous society is a
solution:
The best form of government for a heterogeneous society is a federal system.

It should also be noted that heterogeneity, different languages, customs and levels of political economic and social developments among the tribes play a prominent role in forcing confederal system of government on the people.

question: Capitalism is a system of economic organization based on

option.a: a mixed economy that takes all ineterests into consideration
option.b: individual ownership of the means of production
option.c: very fair distribution of the means of production
option.d: trading among people who own and control their items of trade

solution:
Capitalism is a system of economic organization based on individual ownership of means of production.

It is an economic and political ideology which allows individual ownership and control of the means of production and to produce what they like. The guiding principle is profit maximization.

question: Associations whose main interest is to influence public policies without having to capture power are
solution:
Associations whose main interest is to influence public policy without having to capture power are pressure groups.

Pressure groups are defined as association of people who seek to influence government actions and policies to the benefit of their members.

question: The type of government operated in Nigeria between October 1st 1979 to December 31st 1983 is called a
solution:
The type of government operated in Nigeria between October 1st 1979 to December 31st 1983 is called a presidential system of government.

That was the first time Nigeria started to practice the presidential system. The first executive president was Alhaji Shehu Shagari

question: Financial allocations to local governments by the federal or the state government to supplement the cost of a project is called
solution:
Financial allocations to local government by the federal or the state governments to supplement the cost of a project is called matching grants.

Local governments in turn give matching grants to communities or institutions for completion of certain projects such as schools.

question: The military Head of State during the Third Republic was
solution:
The military Head of State during the Third Republic was General Ibrahim Babangida.

The regime established two political parties, NRC and SDP. All the states had elected governors as well as state legislators. Every local government in the country was also run by elected chairmen and Councillors. That was the time Ibrahim Babangida took up the name ''Mr President.''

question: The 1976 Reforms have been most beneficial to the
solution: The 1976 Local Government Reforms in Nigeria were a landmark initiative aimed at restructuring and empowering local governance. These reforms established local governments as the third tier of Nigeria's federal system, granting them constitutional recognition, defined boundaries, and specific administrative and fiscal responsibilities

question: Nigeria's influence in OPEC is determined by the

option.d: low sulphur content of her crude

solution:
Nigeria's influence in OPEC is determined by volume of oil revenue.

Nigeria has its own trust fund which is estimated at about ninety million naria in the African Development Bank (ADB). From this aid is given to needy countries. This marvelous achievement is due to the volume of Nigeria's oil revenues. This in no small measure boosts the economy of Nigeria.

question: The tenure of the President of the UN Security Council is
solution: The President of the United Nations Security Council serves a tenure of one month, with the position rotating among the 15 member states (5 permanent and 10 non-permanent) in alphabetical order (based on English country names). This monthly rotation ensures equitable representation and shared leadership.

question: Nigeria''s support for the South-South Cooperation is based on her desire to

option.a: assert her leadership role in Africa
option.c: promote economic understanding in the Third World
option.d: counter the political and military domination by major powers

solution: Nigeria's support for South-South Cooperation is rooted in its commitment to fostering economic collaboration and mutual development among developing nations (the "Third World"). This cooperation aims to enhance resource sharing, technology transfer, and collective self-reliance, reducing dependency on the Global North.

question: The countries in which Nigeria participated in the ECOMOG peace-keeping operations were
solution: The countries in which Nigeria participated in the ECOMOG peace-keeping operations were Liberia and Sierra Leone. The ECOMOG helped in restoring democracy in these two countries.

question: Nigeria's relation with black political communities outside Africa is built on

option.b: expectations of political support from them

solution: This reflects the idea that Nigeria's diplomatic and political connections with black communities outside of Africa are often based on a sense of shared cultural heritage, history, and identity.

question: The current Executive Secretary of the ECOWAS is
solution: As of 2004, the Executive Secretary of ECOWAS was Mohammed Ibn Chambers. He served in this role from 2002 to 2006

question: Nigeria demonstrated her commitment to the policy of non-alignment during the regime of
solution: Nigeria’s commitment to the policy of non-alignment—refusing to align with either the Western or Eastern Cold War blocs—was most decisively demonstrated during the regime of General Murtala Muhammed (1975–1976). Under his leadership, Nigeria pursued an assertive foreign policy that prioritized African solidarity and independence from external influence.

question: The two-chambers of elected national representatives in Nigeria are called
solution: The two-chambers of elected national representatives in Nigeria are called the National Assembly. The chambers are House of Representatives and House of Senate.

question: A distinguishing feature of the 1979 Constitution was the

option.c: departure from the parliamentary to the presidential system of government

solution: Departure from the parliamentary to the presidential system of government is the distinguishing feature of the 1979 Constitution . Nigeria practised parliamentary system of government during the first Republic. But the 1979 constitution introduced the presidential system.

question: Judicial administrations in respect of National Code of Conduct lies with the
solution: The Judicial administrations in respect of National Code of Conduct lies with the Code of Conduct Bureau

question: The process of nationalism was accelerated by

option.a: the signing of the Atlantic Charter

solution: The Atlantic Charter (signed in 1941 by Winston Churchill and Franklin D. Roosevelt) accelerated nationalism in colonized territories by explicitly endorsing the principle of self-determination. After World War II, nationalist leaders in Africa and Asia (e.g., Kwame Nkrumah, Jomo Kenyatta) cited the Charter to demand independence, arguing that colonial rule violated the Allies' proclaimed commitment to freedom and equality.

question: The major motivation for British colonization of Nigeria was to
solution: The major motivation for British colonization of Nigeria was to satisfy British economic interest. They wanted a market for their raw and finished products. British did not allow her colonies to trade directly with other countries. Before Nigeria's independence, Britain spelt out the conditions of trade. For instance she determined the prices of Nigerian import and export commodities.

question: The first franchise in the history of the democratic process is
solution:
The first franchise in the history of the democratic process is male franchise. 
This  is a form of voting rights in which all adult males within a political system are allowed to vote, regardless of income, property, religion, race, or any other qualification.

question: A major influence on the formation of public opinion is
solution: Mass media—such as television, radio, newspapers, and social media—plays a crucial role in shaping public opinion by framing issues, setting the public agenda, and influencing the way people think about events and policies. It reaches a wide audience and can reinforce or challenge perceptions, making it a major influence on how opinions are formed.

question: The highest grade in the civil service is known as the
solution: The highest grade in the civil service is known as the administrative cadre

question: In a parliamentary system of government,a vote of no confidence leads to the resignation of
solution: In a parliamentary system, a successful vote of no confidence results in the resignation of both the Prime Minister and the entire cabinet. This occurs because the government (executive) derives its authority from the legislature's support. Losing this support means the government can no longer govern effectively, leading to collective resignation.

question: The economic basis of feudalism is
solution: The economic basis of feudalism is agriculture. Feudalism is a system of government based on land ownership whenever one talks of agriculture he must have to think of land.

question: Unicameral legislature is a common feature of
solution: A unicameral legislature (single legislative chamber) is most commonly associated with unitary systems of government. In unitary states, power is centralized, and subnational units (if they exist) derive authority from the central government. A single legislative body aligns with this simplicity and centralized decision-making.

question: In a presidential system of government, the president checks the legislature through
solution: The president checks the legislature through exercise of veto in a presidential system of government. This was entrenched in the 1979 constitution and remains like that in the 1999 constitution

question: Independence of the judiciary is pertinent because it accords the judiciary the power to

option.a: dismiss any judge who has breached the judicial code of conduct
option.b: determine a fixed salary for judges
option.c: determine a fixed term of office for the judges
option.d: enable the judges to try and decide cases without bias

solution: The independence of the judiciary ensures that judges can adjudicate cases impartially, free from external pressures (e.g., political interference, public opinion, or personal interests). This autonomy is critical to upholding the rule of law, protecting individual rights, and maintaining public trust in legal systems.

question: In the Second Republic,the ruling National Party of Nigeria formed an alliance with the
solution: During the Second Republic, the ruling National Party of Nigeria (NPN) entered into an alliance with the Nigerian People's Party (NPP), popularly known as the NPN-NPP Accord. This alliance was aimed at consolidating power in the National Assembly, although the coalition was often fraught with conflicts.

question: Government-owned companies operating in the economic sector are referred to as
solution: Government-owned companies operating in the economic sector are referred to as public enterprises.

question: The immediate cause of the January 15 1966 military coup in Nigeria was the

option.b: election crisis in the Western Region

solution: The immediate cause of the January 15 1966 military coup in Nigeria was the election crisis in the Western Region

question: The relationship between the tiers of government in Nigeria can be described as one of
solution:
Nigeria’s federal system constitutionally divides powers among three tiers—federal, state, and local governments—granting each autonomy in their respective spheres. For example:

The federal government handles defense, foreign affairs, and currency (Exclusive Legislative List).

States manage education, health, and agriculture (Residual List).

Concurrent areas (e.g., transportation) allow collaboration, though federal law prevails in conflicts.

question: Nigeria supports the South-South cooperation as a foreign policy objective for mutual understanding among
solution: South-South cooperation refers to collaboration among developing countries, particularly in Africa, Asia, and Latin America, to promote economic and political solidarity. Nigeria supports this as part of its foreign policy to foster economic growth, trade and mutual understanding among developing nations rather than just those south of the equator or limited to Sub-Saharan Africa.

question: Nigeria is rated in the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries as the
solution: Nigeria is rated in the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries as the sixth largest oil producer

question: The Nigerian-Cameroon crisis over the Bakassi Peninsula occurred owing to the interpretation of the treaty of 1913 and the

option.d: decision of the International Court of Justice

solution: The Nigerian-Cameroon crisis over the Bakassi Peninsula occurred owing to the interpretation of the treaty of 1913 and the decision of the International Court of Justice

question: An indication that Africa is still the center-peice of Nigeria's foreign policy was the creation of the

option.a: Ministry of Integration and Cooperation in Africa
option.b: National Institute of Policy and Strategic Studies

solution: An indication that Africa is still the center-peice of Nigeria's foreign policy was the creation of the National Institute of Policy and Strategic Studies

question: The atomic bomb tests carried out around the Sahara Desert made Nigeria sever relations with
solution: The atomic bomb tests carried out around the Sahara Desert made Nigeria sever relations with France

question: The political party formed in 1923 by Herbert Macaulay was

option.b: National Council of Nigeria and the Cameroons

solution: Herbert Macaulay, regarded as the father of Nigerian nationalism, founded the Nigerian National Democratic Party (NNDP) in 1923. It was the first political party in Nigeria and aimed to advocate for Nigerian interests under British colonial rule

question: The failure of the assimilation policy necessitated of the introduction of
solution: The failure of the assimilation policy necessitated the introduction of association. The policy of association and assimilation were used by France to administer her colonies.

question: The head of the Nigerian judiciary is the

option.b: President of the Court of Appeal

solution: The Chief Justice of the Federation is Head of Nigerian Judiciary.

question: A major function of the State Independent Electoral Commission in Nigeria is the

option.b: conduct of elections into local government offices

solution: A major function of the State Independent Electoral Commission in Nigeria is the conduct of elections into local government offices. The local government offices are Chairman and Councilors.

question: In a first-past-the-post electoral system, a candidate is declared when he

option.a: records the highest number of votes cast
option.b: obtains a two-thirds majority of the votes cast
option.c: obtains one-third of the votes cast
option.d: scors the aggregates of the opponent

solution: In a first-past-the-post electoral system, a candidate is declared when he records the highest number of votes cast. This system is also know as plurality system or simple majority. In this elected candidate must secure majority of the votes cast.

question: The final stage in the process of enacting legislation is
solution:
The final stage in the process of enacting legislation is assent of the bill.

Third reading: In this final stage, a thorough look would be taken at the bill in order to correct certain errors connected with the drafting or amendment. A final vote is also taken on the bill at this stage. The bill now taken to the President for his assent after the bill had passed all the five stages is signed by the President.

question: The study of government essentially facilitates the understanding of the

option.a: functioning of the entire social formation
option.b: organization of the executive arm of government

solution: The study of government will shield  us from ignorance towards the body governing our country. It is really important for anyone to know, how his/her country is being managed. Of course, when we know a lot about the systems governing our country, then, we will also know our responsibilities as a citizen of that country, enlightens citizens on their basic fundamental human rights and avoid the abuse of such rights.

question: Serfs are the dominated class under
solution: Serfs are the dominated class under feudalism. The first class is the king who was regarded as the sole owner of the land. The Serfs were the actual cultivators of the land.

question: The most important aspect of political participation in a democracy is
solution:
voting in elections is the most important aspect of political participation in a democracy.

Membership of a political party makes one to see it as a must to vote at elections and also fights vigorously to see that others vote for his party.

He endeavors to attend many political rallies. A person with good conscience tries as much as possible to observe most electoral rules.

The person also stands the good chance of contesting at elections.

question: Under a parliamentary system of government, the cabinet holds office at the pleasure of the
solution:
Under a parliamentary system of government,the cabinet holds office at the pleasure of the legislature.

Under the parliamentary system of government, the Prime Minister is the Head of government while the King, Queen or ceremonial President is the Head of State.

The Chairman or leader of the party that won the highest number of seats in the legislature becomes the Prime Minister. He, the Prime Minister forms the government according to the constitution.

The Prime Minister can only choose his ministers from his party men in the legislature. The executive can be removed from office by the legislature. This could happen if the legislature passes a vote of no confidence in the executive.

question: The first successful disengagement of the military from politics in Nigeria was carried out by the government of
solution: The first successful disengagement of the military from politics in Nigeria occurred under the Murtala/Obasanjo regime (1975–1979). This transition culminated in the handover of power to a democratically elected civilian government on October 1, 1979, marking the end of military rule and the beginning of Nigeria’s Second Republic

question: In local government administration, Chief and Council implies that

option.a: the council operates independently of the chief authority
option.b: members of the local council appoints the chief to the office
option.c: all members of the local council are appointed and controlled by the chief
option.d: the chief is a member of the local council but he does not exercise veto power

solution: In many traditional local government systems, especially in contexts like First Nations governance, the structure referred to as "Chief and Council" means that the chief is elected as part of the council. While the chief often acts as a symbolic or coordinating leader, he does not have unilateral control or veto power over the council's decisions. Instead, decisions are made collectively by the council, ensuring a balance of power among its members. This distinguishes the "Chief and Council" model from systems where one leader might have overriding authority over an independent council.

question: A key characteristics of the civil service is
solution:
A key characteristic of the civil service is Merit System.

Recruitment into the Civil Service should be based on merit and not on quota or favouritism.

This is to enable the Civil Servants to perform their functions very well and then ensure the success of the government.

question: The right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences is
solution:
Franchise the right that enables citizens to express their electoral preferences.

It should be noted that there are two types of franchise which are as follows:

1. Restricted Franchise or Limited Franchise

2. Unlimited Franchise or Universal Adult Suffrage.

question: Equality before the law is a component of
solution: "Equality before the law" is a core component of the rule of law, which ensures that all individuals and institutions—including the government—are subject to and accountable under the law. This principle mandates that laws are applied impartially, without favoritism or discrimination based on status, wealth, or power.

question: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the
solution: An institution that transforms a myriad of demands into a more manageable proposal is the Government.

question: A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called
solution:
A system of government that promotes the rights and freedom of the citizens is called Democracy.

Democracy is a government where people exercise power of governance through representatives. The representative must come into govern through popular, periodic free and fair elections.

According to Abraham Lincoin, ''democracy is the government of the people, by the people and for the people.''

question: In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs
solution:
In the process of implementing laws, the executive sometimes performs Judicial Functions.

1. The executive can grant prerogative of mercy to convicts, especially those that have overstayed or stayed reasonable part of their term in detention.

2. They executive also sets up judicial panels of inquiry outside the control of the ordinary courts of the land.

question: Which of the following justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria?

option.a: Her location in the West African sub-region
option.b: Her colonization by a foriegn power
option.c: Her physical size, population and resource endowments
option.d: Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions

solution:
Her ability to contribute to peace keeping missions justifies the Afro-centric foreign policy of Nigeria.

was highly committed to peaceful settlement of political crises and inter state disputes.

She played very prominent role in the settlement of political crises in Liberia and Sierra Leone through the formation of ECOMOG.

question: Nigeria hosted the Commonwealth conference which eventually led to the independence of
solution:
Nigeria hosted the Commonwealth conference which eventually led to the independence of Zimbabwe.

When Nigeria was plunging very deep into political crises, the Nigerian Prime Minister, Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa preferred to focus all attention on the Rhodesian crisis. The country actually hosted a Commonwealth meeting on the Rhodesian question till the 15th of January, 1966.

question: The first military coup in Nigeria toppled the government of
solution: The first military coup in Nigeria occurred on January 15, 1966, led by a group of army officers (including Major Chukwuma Nzeogwu). This coup overthrew the First Republic civilian government headed by Prime Minister Sir Abubakar Tafewa Balewa, who was killed during the coup.

question: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for
solution: Adjudication of conflict between the federal and regional governments in the First Republic was reserved for Supreme Court

question: Prior to 1966,the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of

option.a: native authority, division and district councils

solution: Prior to 1966, the system of local government in western region was based on the three-tier structure of Division, District and Local Councils. In Western Nigeria, there was the 1952 ordinance which established three tier system---divisional, district and local councils. Each tier was given responsibilities according to its resources, with the divisional councils shouldering the greatest responsibilities.

question: A major shortcoming of the Lyttelton Constitution was that

option.a: the Governor-General continued to head the government
option.b: no provision was made for the office of the prime minister
option.c: it failed to provide for the office of a premier
option.d: A major shortcoming of the Lyttelton Constitution was that

solution: The Lyttleton Constitution of 1954, while introducing Nigeria’s first federal structure, faced criticism for failing to establish the office of a Prime Minister at the federal level. Instead, executive authority remained vested in the Governor-General, who chaired the Federal Executive Council and retained veto powers over legislation

question: The option A-4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the
solution:
The option A-4 voting system was introduced into the political process in Nigeria during the Third Republic.

It was introduced by the then Chairman of the National Electoral Commission (N.E.C), Professor Humphrey Nwosu.

question: In orientation, early nationalism in Nigeria was essentially
solution: In the context of early nationalism in Nigeria, the orientation was generally reformist. Early nationalism in Nigeria was often a response to the socio-political and economic conditions imposed by colonial rule. The nationalist movements sought to address grievances and push for reforms in response to the challenges posed by colonialism rather than aiming for a revolutionary or radical overhaul.

question: Which of the following formalized colonial penetration into Africa?
solution: The Berlin Conference (1884–1885) formalized European colonial penetration into Africa. Convened by Otto von Bismarck, it established rules for the partition of Africa among European powers, leading to the Scramble for Africa

question: Which of the following is saddled with the conduct of the general elections in Nigeria's Fourth Republic?
solution: The Fourth Republic is the current republican government of Nigeria. Since 1999 it has governed the country according to the fourth republican constitution. INEC conducts elections

question: Privatization and commercialization programmes in Nigeria have
solution:
Privatization and commercialization programs in Nigeria were initiated to reduce the government's direct involvement in business enterprises. By transferring ownership and management of public enterprises to private entities, these programs aimed to enhance efficiency, stimulate economic growth, and alleviate the financial burden on the government. This strategic shift marked a significant move away from extensive government ownership toward a more market-driven economy.​

While these programs have had varied impacts, including debates on their influence on government revenue and concerns about corruption, their primary characteristic has been the reduction of government ownership in favor of private sector participation.

question: In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the
solution:
In the Igbo political system, the most senior member of the council of elders is the Okpara. The Igbo political system was based on the village as a political unit.

A village is then seen as made up of a group of related families. Each family head held the Ofo title and all of them put together formed the council of elders. The council of elders governed the village. Among the Ofo title holders, one of them was regarded as the most senior to as the Okpara and he had the right to preside over the council of elders meetings.

question: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was
solution: The immediate cause of the 1966 military coup de'etat in Nigeria was political instability. It should be noted that the Action Group Crises of 1962 was one of the crisis that led to the military take-over of government in January 1966. The 1962 Action Group crisis continued to rear its ugly head, until Western Nigeria election crisis of 1965. The disagreement over the election result between UPGA and NNA in Western region resulted to a crisis which led to a breakdown of law and order. This situation attracted military intervention on 15th January, 1966.

question: The participation of Nigerians in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the

option.b: Africans were more qualified to rule
option.d: Europeans were less tactful in warfare

solution:
The Participation of Nigerians in the Second World War created in the nationalists the awareness that the Europeans were not superior.

Their participation in the war, made them to know more about the white people, and realized that they were in no way superior to Africans including Nigerians.

question: Prior to 1976, the local government system practiced in Northern Nigeria was
solution: Prior to 1976, the Local government system practised in Northern Nigerian Nigeria was Chief-in-Council.By 1952, the Northern region abolished the sole Native Authority and put in its place, Chief-in-Council and Chief-and-Council and. The democratization process in the Northern region was further aided by the Native Authority Law of 1954 which was amended by the Northern Nigeria Law of 1963. They provided for five Native Authorities including Chief-in-Council and the Federal Native Authorities.

question: Which of the following influenced nationalist activities in Nigeria?

option.d: National Congress of British West Africa

solution: In the history of Nigerian nationalism, regional organizations played a significant role in mobilizing and uniting educated elites and activists against colonial rule. The National Congress of British West Africa was one such organization that brought together nationalist leaders from across the British West African colonies, including Nigeria, to demand self-governance and greater political representation. This organization provided both a model and inspiration for the emerging nationalist activities in Nigeria.

question: The party that did not control any state in the Second Republic was
solution: NAP was the party that did not control any state in the Second Republic. Nigeria Advanced Party (NAP) was not recognized for the 1979 election. The reason was that it was unable to establish branches in 13 states of the federation as stipulated by FEDECO. In the 1983 general elections, which the party contested, it did not win a single seat in all the constituencies.

question: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for
solution: An advantage of flexible constitution is that it allows for ease of amendment. Changes can be made easily in order to accommodate changing situations in the society. Its amendment does not require or involve much time and financial cost.

question: Occupational pressure groups pursue their objectives mainly by

option.a: issuing ultimatums and embarking on strikes

solution: Occupational pressure groups pursue their objectives mainly by pursue their objectives mainly by issuing ultimatums and embarking on strikes. The pressure groups give, 7, 14 or 21 days ultimatum to the government at the end of this, any action which the group deems necessary will be taken against the government. If the demands of the pressure groups are not met at the end of the ultimatum given, they will have to embark on strikes and boycotts.

question: In a cabinet system, the legislature comprises of the

option.a: prime minister and members of the parliament
option.b: prime minister and members of the jury

solution:
In a cabinet system, the legislature comprises the prime minister and members of the parliament.

One of the characteristics of cabinet system of government is that the Prime Minister and all cabinet ministers are also members of the parliament or legislature.

question: The commitee that scrutinizes budget proposals in the legislature is the
solution: The Committee that scrutinizes budget proposals in the legislature is the Appropriation Committee.

question: Democracy is a system of government that is

option.a: constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens
option.b: imposed on the majority of the citizens
option.d: put in place to favour the party caucus

solution:
Democracy is a system of government that is constituted by the will of the majority of the citizens.

It could also be defined as government in accordance with the principles of popular sovereignty, political equality, popular consultation and majority rule. One of the characteristics of democracy is that all government actions must be open to criticisms and must be according to the decisions, wishes and aspirations of majority of the people.

question: Collective ownership of property is associated with
solution:
Collective ownership of property is associated with Communism .

It should be noted that Communism is a social system where the means of production, distribution and exchange are completely owned and managed by the State. The economy is operated centrally and individuals have little or no opportunity for private enterprises.
